title: Mrs N Jaafri v Marks & Spencer plc: 1601288/2022
text:
Case Number: 1601288/2022 
EMPLOYMENT TRIBUNALS 
Claimant: 
Respondent: 
HELD AT: 
BEFORE:  
Mrs N Jaafri 
Marks & Spencer PLC 
Mold  
on: 7th November 2024  
Employment Judge T. Vincent Ryan 
Ms C Peel 
Mr B Roberts 
REPRESENTATION: 
Claimant: A Litigant in Person 
Interpreter (Arabic/English): Mr A Shaltout 
Respondent: Mr D Evans, Counsel 
JUDGMENT  
The unanimous judgment of the Tribunal is that: 
1. The Claimant’s claim of beach of contract (notice), or alternatively of an 
Unauthorised Deduction from Wages, is dismissed upon withdrawal, the 
Respondent having honoured the Claimant’s entitlement to net notice pay. 
2.  The following claims fail and are dismissed: 
2.1. Unfair Dismissal, the Claimant having been fairly dismissed by letter dated 19 
August 2022 for a reason related to incapability by reference to her health. 
2.2. Direct Race Discrimination. 
2.3. Disability Discrimination (the claims of a failure to make reasonable 
adjustments, to provide an auxiliary aid, and a claim of discrimination arising 
from disability). 
2.4. Victimisation, albeit the Claimant did a protected act when she raised a 
written grievance on 26 October 2021. 
1 
  Case Number: 1601288/2022 
 
 2 
     Employment Judge T.V. Ryan 
     Date: 07.11.24 
      
     JUDGMENT SENT TO THE PARTIES ON 
 
11 November 2024       
 
 Katie Dickson 
 
                                                                         FOR THE TRIBUNAL OFFICE 
 
 
Note 
Reasons for the judgment having been given orally at the hearing, written reasons will not be provided 
unless a request was made by either party at the hearing (and no such request was made) or a 
written request is presented by either party within 14 days of the sending of this written record of the 
decision.
